hook: The US-China frontier model gap is down to 2.7%
caption: Stanford's 2026 AI Index says the US-China frontier gap has effectively closed, down to ~2.7% and Chinese models now trade the lead. The race you thought was settled isn't. The stuff my feed keeps.
stance: The US lead in frontier models is basically a rounding error now.

hook: Anthropic says its new model is too dangerous to release
caption: Anthropic says Mythos is too dangerous to ship freely — so it's teaming with rivals to secure it. A lab holding back its own model is the story of the year. Found before the timeline.
stance: The scariest AI headline isn't a leak — it's a lab refusing to release.

hook: Frontier AI labs quietly killed the release milestone
caption:
Frontier AI labs quietly killed the release milestone.

2023-24 had clear moments: GPT-4, Claude 3, Gemini 1.5. Mid-2025 flipped to silent rolling updates. The stuff my feed keeps.
stance: Version numbers are dead; the model you used yesterday isn't the one you're using now.

hook: Hire the problem solver — you can't teach that later.
caption: This game dev hires for one thing: thoughtful problem solvers. Technical skills you can teach anyone. Analytical thinking you can't. 162 likes agree. The takes my feed keeps before the timeline buries them.
stance: Skills are trainable; judgment isn't — hire for the thing you can't install.

hook: Objective reality might not exist. A real experiment says so.
caption: Objective reality might not exist. A quantum test let two observers record different "true" outcomes of the same event. Physics-breaking stuff my feed pulls before the timeline finds it.
stance: If two observers can both be right about contradictory facts, "objective" was always a comforting story.

hook: "Negative time" is real. Photons exited before they entered.
caption: "Negative time" is real: photons left a material before they finished entering it. Not a glitch, a measured result. The wait-what science my feed keeps and the noise it drops.

hook: Molecules big enough to see just existed in two places at once.
caption: The biggest molecules ever were just put in two places at once. Quantum weirdness is climbing toward our scale. Where does it stop? The wait-what findings my feed pulls first.

hook: AI is designing quantum experiments no human ever imagined.
caption: An AI built to crunch numbers is now inventing quantum experiments physicists couldn't conceive. The tool started leading the science. Found before the timeline, kept by my feed.
stance: We built AI to speed up our ideas. It's quietly having its own.

hook: Reality doesn't exist until you measure it.
caption: Reality doesn't exist until you measure it. Australian physicists re-ran a famous quantum experiment and confirmed it. The stuff my feed keeps while the timeline scrolls past.
stance: If observation creates reality, "objective" physics is doing a lot of heavy lifting.

hook: Physicists just measured light spending "negative time"
caption: Physicists measured light spending "negative time" inside a material. It exits before it should have entered. Your intuition is wrong and the math is right. The stuff my feed keeps.
stance: "Negative time" isn't a glitch or a headline trick -- it's a real measured delay, and it breaks how you picture cause and effect.

hook: Heat just flowed cold-to-hot. The arrow of time reversed.
caption: In a quantum system, heat flowed backward -- cold to hot -- reversing the arrow of time. Everything you were taught about entropy assumes this can't happen. The stuff my feed keeps.
stance: The second law of thermodynamics feels absolute until a quantum system runs it in reverse.

hook: Alibaba says its new model beats DeepSeek.
caption: Alibaba says its new model beats DeepSeek. China's model war just got a second front, and the West isn't in this fight. The release my feed flagged before the timeline noticed.
stance: The real frontier race is now Alibaba vs DeepSeek, not OpenAI vs Google.

hook: OpenAI delayed a model release after a request from the Trump administration.
caption: OpenAI staggered its GPT-5.6 preview after a Trump administration request. Model releases are national policy now. Found this before the timeline turned it into a war.
stance: Frontier releases now clear political review before they clear you.

hook: She broke the AI-sentience story 5 months before ChatGPT
caption: Four years ago she broke the Blake Lemoine 'sentient AI' story — 5 months before ChatGPT existed. Now Anthropic, Google and Meta hire scientists to study it. The threads that age well, kept.
stance: The guy everyone mocked in 2022 was early, not wrong.

hook: Companies are making Claude talk like a caveman to save tokens
caption: Companies are tuning AI to talk like a terse caveman, not a polite chatbot — on purpose, to stop burning tokens. Every 'please' costs money now. The weird AI economics my feed catches.
stance: Politeness in prompts is a line item, and it's getting cut.

hook: Google's Genie can generate games. Game stocks just dropped.
caption: Google's Genie can generate playable worlds from a prompt — and videogame stocks slid the same day. The market is pricing in AI. Found before the timeline caught it.
stance: Generative game models are a bigger threat to studios than anyone's admitting.

hook: Alibaba just dropped 100+ open-source AI models at once.
caption: Alibaba shipped over 100 new open-source AI models in one move — text, image, the works. The open-weight race isn't slowing down. The stuff my feed keeps.
stance: The best open models won't come from the West.

hook: White House just lifted its export ban on Anthropic's AI.
caption: Weeks after freezing foreign access, the White House reversed course on exporting Anthropic's advanced models. AI policy is whiplash right now. The stuff my feed keeps.
stance: AI export controls are being written and rewritten faster than the models ship.

hook: You've been renting your AI. This is what owning it looks like.
caption: Osaurus runs local models: no account, no API key, no Electron, fully open source. You own the whole stack. This is the stuff my feed keeps.
stance: If your AI needs an API key, you don't own it — you're renting.

hook: Bettors give GPT-5.6 a 90% chance to ship by July 31
caption: Polymarket gives GPT-5.6 a 90% chance to drop by July 31, but only 1% before June 30. The market is timing your next model before OpenAI announces it. Found before the timeline.

hook: Anthropic's Mythos model has people worried
caption: Anthropic's new Mythos model already has ex-founders raising alarms on CBS. When the builders get nervous, you pay attention. This is the signal my feed pulls before the noise buries it.
stance: The people closest to frontier models are the most scared of them.

hook: Microsoft is building AI to undercut OpenAI
caption: Microsoft is quietly shipping in-house models to undercut its own partner OpenAI. Your cheapest AI provider might soon be the one funding the competition. Caught this before the timeline did.
stance: Microsoft doesn't need OpenAI anymore — it needs a cheaper OpenAI.

hook: Model launches used to be events. Now they're rolling.
caption: Frontier labs quietly killed the big launch — models now ship continuously, not as events. If you still wait for release day, you're already behind. The shift my feed caught early.
stance: The era of the AI keynote is over — updates arrive silently now.

hook: Anthropic built AI too risky to release publicly
caption: ABC News confirms it: Anthropic built a model it deems too dangerous to release. When a lab won't ship its best work, that tells you where we are. Caught before the timeline.

hook: Anthropic just delayed a Claude model over safety
caption: Anthropic just delayed "Claude Mythos" over security concerns. A frontier lab hitting pause is the signal you actually want to track. Found before the timeline caught it.

caption: GPT-5.2 is out: better spreadsheets, slides, code, and image reads in one model. If it does your busywork, that changes your day, not just a benchmark. Found before the timeline.

caption: Microsoft revealed seven of its own models, IQ, Scout, MDASH, Majorana 2, going full-stack instead of leaning on OpenAI. The dependency era is ending. Found before the timeline.

caption: Google DeepMind's Gemma 4 12B ditches the three-model stack and processes images, sound, and text together. Fewer moving parts, cheaper to run. Found before the timeline.

Microsoft's AI CEO just unveiled 7 new models with one rule: don't replace people.

Mustafa Suleyman calls it Humanist Superintelligence at Build 2026. A direct shot at the everyone-gets-automated crowd. The stuff my feed keeps.

caption: 3,200 jobs cut, 4 studios under new management, zero closed on paper. The layoff math the industry doesn't spell out. The signal my feed keeps before the timeline spins it.

hook: "Women aren't even shallow" — Nietzsche's cruelest line
caption: "Women are considered profound. Why? Because we never fathom their depths. But women aren't even shallow." Nietzsche at his most savage. The line my feed pulled before the doomscroll buried it.
stance: Half of Nietzsche's edgelord bangers are just him losing an argument with himself.

stance: A discipline that tolerates being told you're plainly wrong stays honest longer.

hook: Time isn't fundamental. It emerges from entanglement.

hook: Some problems need an "exotic" quantum proof. No document will do.

hook: A spooky quantum experiment just proved Einstein wrong.
caption: Measuring a photon changes where it is. The 'spooky' experiment Einstein hated keeps winning. Found before the timeline caught on.
stance: Einstein was right about almost everything and wrong about the one thing that mattered most.

hook: Spectroscopy sounds nerdy. It's the study of everything.
caption: Spectroscopy sounds nerdy, but it's how we learned the universe runs on quantum rules before anyone told us. Quietly the study of everything. The stuff my feed keeps.

hook: Einstein called it "spooky." This experiment proved it real.
caption: Einstein hated quantum entanglement -- he called it "spooky action at a distance." A Griffith University experiment just verified it. He was right about the spookiness, wrong to doubt it. Found before the timeline.
stance: The smartest physicist who ever lived bet against entanglement and lost.

hook: A light particle left a cloud before it entered it.
caption: A light particle exited a cloud before it entered -- scientists measured a negative amount of time inside. Quantum physics melts your brain and it's supposed to. Found before the timeline.
stance: The weirdest results in physics aren't errors -- they're the universe telling you your intuition was never built for this.

hook: Quantum proofs beat classical ones. It's now proven.
caption: After 20 years, researchers have strong evidence quantum proofs are strictly more powerful than classical ones -- no way around the quantum world's complexity. This is a big deal for computing. Found before the timeline.
stance: There are truths a classical computer can verify but never efficiently -- and quantum machines just walked past that wall.

hook: Physicists just measured negative time in a quantum experiment.
caption: Physicists measured 'negative time' — photons that exit a cloud before they finish entering it. Not a typo, a real result. The wait-what finding my feed pulled first.
stance: Negative time sounds fake until you realize causality was always more negotiable than you were taught.

hook: Time might not be fundamental — just entanglement in disguise.
caption: Scientists reversed local entropy with quantum computers, hinting time is an emergent property of entanglement, not a law. Physics is more flexible than you were told. Found before the timeline.
stance: Time's arrow isn't a rule of the universe — it's a side effect of how things get entangled.

hook: Hannah Arendt: you don't actually want truth, you want to stop not-knowing

stance: Most of what people call 'seeking truth' is just discomfort with open questions.

hook: Philosophy runs on far-fetched scenarios — with a straight face.

stance: The weirdest thought experiments do the most real philosophical work.

hook: "We are strong so our burdens stay light." — Stoicism.

stance: Stoicism isn't about avoiding hardship — it's about outgrowing it.

hook: Want to write well? Epictetus gave the whole method.
caption: Want to write well? Epictetus: "If you would be a good reader, read; if a writer, write." No hack. Just the loop. Stoic gold my feed keeps.
stance: Every writing course is a footnote to one Stoic line.

hook: You quote the isms — but have you ever practiced them?
caption: You quote the isms. But have you practiced them in the purest form? "Reading is fundamental. When did we stop pushing that idea?" The take my feed kept.

hook: Marcus Aurelius: stop imagining, stop pulling your own strings.

stance: Half your anxiety is imagination pretending to be planning.

hook: Writing like John Locke now reads as anti-intellectual

stance: Live-service mandates killed one of the best single-player studios in gaming.

hook: One-man studio hit with 55k refunds for a 2-hour game
caption: One dev, a finished game, and 55k refunds because Steam's 2-hour rule let players beat it and refund it. Players even left positive reviews while cashing out. The indie stories my feed keeps.
stance: Steam's refund window quietly punishes short, complete indie games for being short and complete.

hook: DeFi hacks are becoming a hidden tax on your yield
caption: High DeFi yields are quietly funding the hacks that drain them — a hidden liquidity tax you pay without seeing it. BTC's at $58.5K and bearish. The read my feed kept.
stance: Your APY isn't yield — it's a hack subsidy waiting to be claimed.

hook: Crypto is shifting from scarcity to real yield-bearing cashflows
caption: Crypto is shifting from 'number go up' scarcity to protocols that throw off real cashflow. AAVE is becoming an investment-grade primitive. The thesis my feed surfaced before the timeline.
stance: Scarcity was the pitch. Yield is the product. AAVE already made the jump.

hook: Time might wobble. And that finally makes gravity testable.
caption: Post-quantum gravity says time itself wobbles, and unlike string theory, you can actually test it. A rival theory of everything you can falsify. The science my feed keeps.
stance: String theory promised everything and tested nothing. This one bets on being provable.

hook: Reality doesn't exist until it's measured, says the ANU.
caption: Reality doesn't exist until it's measured, an ANU quantum experiment finds. 227 likes, 239 replies arguing about it. Found before the timeline caught on.

hook: Most 'quantum computing' headlines are describing machines that don't exist.
caption: The quantum computers in the headlines are almost always theoretical. The ones that exist are tiny. A researcher's reality check my feed kept.
stance: Half of 'quantum breakthrough' coverage is describing hardware nobody has built yet.

hook: Gravity probably isn't what collapses the quantum world.
caption: A leading theory said gravity forces quantum states to 'pick' reality. New experiment says: unlikely. 127 likes deep in the debate. Found before the timeline.

hook: A quantum experiment just simulated a working time machine.
caption: A quantum experiment just simulated a working time machine. Not sci-fi — a real lab result on closed timelike curves. The kind of paper my feed keeps and the timeline buries.
stance: Time travel isn't banned by physics — it's banned by our budget for qubits.

hook: Ask physicists if quantum mechanics needs imaginary numbers — they split 50/50.
caption: Ask physicists if quantum mechanics needs imaginary numbers, and they split 50/50. One of the deepest questions in the field has no consensus. Found before the timeline noticed.
stance: If the experts can't agree whether i is real, your intuition about quantum was never the problem.

hook: Big Tech didn't grow. It bought its way there.
caption: Big Tech didn't grow big. It bought its way there — hundreds of acquisitions, quietly. You use half of them and never knew. The buried context my feed keeps for you.
stance: Most 'innovation' at scale is just acquisition with a press release.

hook: Microsoft is racing ahead while Big Tech pulls back on M&A.
caption: Everyone else is slowing acquisitions this year — Microsoft is racing ahead. When one giant buys while the rest freeze, watch what they're building. Signal my feed caught early.
stance: The M&A slowdown isn't caution — it's everyone waiting to see what Microsoft eats next.

hook: Why don't Big Tech companies actually compete?
caption: Antitrust, lobbying, dark patterns, selling at a loss to kill rivals — why don't Big Tech giants really compete? A question worth sitting with. The threads my feed keeps for you.
stance: 'Free market' and 'Big Tech' haven't been in the same sentence honestly for a decade.

hook: 350+ Big Tech mergers. Zero raised alarms.
caption: 350+ mergers by Facebook and Google — not one got serious scrutiny. The roots of Big Tech run deeper than you think. The stuff my feed keeps before it trends.
stance: Antitrust didn't fail Big Tech — it never showed up.

hook: Every acquisition GAFAM ever made, mapped.
caption: Someone graphed every known Big Tech acquisition into one giant web. It's bigger than you'd guess. This is the deep-dive stuff my feed surfaces before the timeline.

hook: FTC votes 5-0 to reopen Big Tech's past mergers.
caption: Unanimous. Special orders to review deals by Alphabet, Amazon, Apple, Facebook. The mergers nobody stopped are back on the table. Caught before the timeline.

hook: The algorithms rewarding lies also reward the wildfires.
caption: Big Tech's attention economy pays to broadcast lies no matter the cost — and the same engines quietly reward climate disinfo. The uncomfortable link my feed keeps.
stance: The recommendation algorithm isn't neutral — it's optimized for whatever burns hottest.

hook: Big Tech and authoritarians run the same playbook: control what you see.
caption: Both authoritarians and Big Tech want to control the narratives you see — so you never notice them amassing power. The take my feed keeps over the noise.
stance: Feed control is power control. Whoever owns your timeline owns your attention.

hook: The EU isn't waiting — Big Tech antitrust enforcement is live.
caption: 92 likes, 107 replies: while the US stalls, the EU pushes ahead on Big Tech antitrust. The giants you build on are getting reined in overseas first. Caught before the timeline.
stance: The EU regulates Big Tech while the US holds hearings. Guess who wins.

hook: Every 2024 AI model release on one scrollable timeline.
caption: Every 2024 AI model release on one scrollable timeline. The year the pace broke your brain, laid out flat. The kind of thing my feed surfaces before you go looking.

hook: Meta built an AI model that grades other AI models' work
caption: Meta released an AI model whose job is checking other AI models' work. AI reviewing AI. The self-supervising loop starts here — the releases my feed keeps before they scroll away.
stance: The next models won't be trained by humans — they'll be graded by other models.

hook: AI sovereignty is now a hardware problem, not a software one
caption: AI sovereignty just became a hardware problem — Huawei's 100x scaling roadmap signals who owns the chips owns the models. The economics story under the hype, kept by my feed.
stance: Whoever controls the silicon controls the AI, and it isn't the US by default.

hook: A new AI reads stress from your brain at 82.78% accuracy.
caption: A Riemannian Manifold Attention Network hit 82.78% accuracy detecting stress from EEG signals — beating prior benchmarks. Brain-reading AI is quietly getting good. Found before the timeline.

hook: Frontier models now ship monthly, like continuous deployment
caption: Monthly frontier releases mean AI has stopped shipping versions and started shipping like CI/CD. Blink and your stack is a generation behind. The stuff my feed keeps so you don't miss a drop.
stance: Model releases are now a deploy cadence, not an event.

hook: The fastest-growing AI repos this week aren't the ones you starred
caption: The fastest-growing AI repos this week aren't the ones you starred. Awesome-evals is quietly blowing up while everyone chases models. This is the kind of signal my feed keeps.
stance: Eval tooling is the real AI infra story, not another model drop.

hook: Microsoft still thinks AAA gaming is a growth industry
caption: MS is betting AAA is still growth, not just aging millennial fans. The bet driving every Xbox layoff and reshuffle you're watching. The take my feed keeps before it's obvious.
stance: Treating AAA as a growth market in 2026 is how you justify the cuts that kill it.

hook: Sony is gutting gaming while you watch Microsoft
caption: Sony is gutting gaming while everyone watches Microsoft. The timing isn't a coincidence — it's cover. Industry moves like this surface in my feed before the timeline catches on.
stance: The Microsoft news is a smokescreen — Sony picked this exact week on purpose.

hook: GDC is fading. Gamescom is eating its lunch.
caption: GDC is fading while Gamescom goes from strength to strength. The center of gravity in gaming events is shifting — this is the kind of signal my feed keeps and the timeline misses.

hook: Assassin's Creed Black Flag is getting resynced
caption: AC Black Flag Resynced lands July 9 on PS5 and Xbox — the pirate era, back. If Black Flag was your favorite Assassin's Creed, mark the date. Caught before the timeline.

hook: Sony's PS disc factory is already being repurposed
caption: Sony's PS disc factory is already being repurposed, and Microsoft's lining up buyers for Xbox studios. Physical media and the console era are quietly ending — the signal my feed keeps.
stance: The disc era is over and nobody's holding a funeral.

hook: "Indie gaming will save the industry" is a myth
caption: "Indie gaming will save the industry" is a myth, and one dev just said it out loud. The comforting takes rarely survive scrutiny. The contrarian threads my feed keeps before they blow up.
stance: Indies are a genre, not a rescue plan for a $200B industry.

hook: 15 biggest game reveals from July 2026, in one place
caption: 15 biggest game reveals from July 2026, in one place. Every major announcement and gameplay drop from the past few weeks. Skip the doomscroll — the roundup my feed built for you.

hook: The Dreamcast still gets new games in 2026
caption: The Dreamcast still gets new physical indie releases every year — 25 years after it died. No AAA studio can kill a scene that loves the hardware. The niche gems my feed keeps.
stance: Dead consoles have healthier release schedules than half of AAA.

hook: 96% of indie games make no money
caption: 96% of indie games make almost nothing — including ones you loved. The dream is real, the odds are brutal. The hard numbers my feed surfaces before you sink 3 years in.
stance: Passion isn't a business model — most indie devs learn that too late.

hook: PlayStation just killed physical indie releases
caption: A dev who shipped a physical indie game calls it one of PlayStation's worst decisions ever. Collectors and players still want discs. The industry takes my feed keeps for you.
stance: Killing physical media is killing the games that outlive their servers.

hook: 1 year of game dev, zero prior experience
caption: A full year of solo game dev with no prior experience, compressed to 30 seconds — built entirely in Godot. Proof the barrier dropped. The maker stuff my feed keeps.
stance: You don't need a studio or a degree — you need Godot and a year.

hook: Indie devs don't owe you a $100 deluxe edition.
caption: Indie devs don't owe you a $100 deluxe edition. Build a kick-ass product and players show up. The take my feed kept out of 5,000 posts about game launches.
stance: The best indie games ship without a collector's edition, not despite it.

hook: Steam and GoG killed the physical game disc.
caption: Steam and GoG made libraries so trustworthy nobody misses discs anymore. Most indie devs won't press one again. The debate my feed pulled from the launch-day noise.
stance: Physical PC releases are dead and nobody actually cares.

hook: An atmospheric driving game through Dutch polders and windmills.
caption: An atmospheric driving game through Dutch dikes, polders and windmills, built in public in Unity. The weird gem my feed keeps while the timeline chases AAA trailers.

hook: The game that made you a dev is already unplayable
caption: The game that made you fall in love with games is probably already gone — pulled, delisted, unplayable 12 years later. Digital gaming forgets fast. The stuff my feed keeps.
stance: Delisting is quiet cultural erasure — we're losing the games that made us.

hook: Marcus Aurelius on why you keep misreading people
caption: "Direct thy attention to what is said. Let thy understanding enter into the things that are doing." Marcus Aurelius on actually paying attention. Stoicism the feed kept before it scrolled past.

hook: Studying philosophy turned out to be absurdly useful
caption: "Studying philosophy turned out to be consistently, ridiculously useful for me." Not the pitch you were sold about the useless degree. The quiet contrarian take my feed kept for you.
stance: Philosophy is the most practical degree nobody will admit is practical.

hook: There's a phenomenology of lying — banal vs non-banal.
caption: A free philosophy journal breaks down 'narrative breakdown' — a full phenomenology of banal and non-banal lying. The niche gems my feed surfaces before the timeline ever shows them.
stance: Not all lies are equal — some are structurally worse than others.

hook: The city you walk shapes how you think.
caption: Psychogeography: the psychological impact of urban space on your mind. Merlin Coverley's book, reviewed. Where philosophy meets the streets you actually live in. The stuff my feed keeps before the timeline buries it.
stance: Your environment edits your thoughts more than you notice.

hook: The Declaration of Independence is applied philosophy.
caption: The Founders were steeped in one question: defining Natural Law. That's why the DOI matters — inalienable rights and consent of the governed are Enlightenment principles. Found before the timeline.
stance: The DOI is a philosophy paper that happened to found a country.

hook: Plato: obsessing over health blocks the practice of virtue.
caption: Plato: "Attention to health is a great obstacle to the practice of virtue." A shot at wellness culture 2,400 years early. The take my feed kept.
stance: Optimizing your body can become a way to avoid improving your character.

hook: "The music that can deepest reach is cordial speech."
caption: "The music that can deepest reach, and cure all ill, is cordial speech." Emerson, on why kind words outlast every argument you'll win online. One line my feed kept out of the noise.

hook: All of ancient Chinese political thought in one messy chart
caption: Daoism, Legalism, and the rest of ancient Chinese political thought mapped in one gloriously messy hand-drawn chart. Confusion cleared. A gem my feed pulled out of 5,000 posts.

hook: Your game engine has no idea what beer is
caption: Your game engine has no idea what beer is. A solo dev's devlog on teaching a simulation game the logic behind brewing, from scratch. The build-in-public stuff my feed keeps.
stance: The hard part of sim games isn't graphics, it's modeling systems the engine knows nothing about.

hook: Godot 4.8 Dev1 just dropped a sneak peek
caption: Godot 4.8 Dev1 just dropped, and it's an early look at what's landing in the next release. If you build in the open-source engine devs actually love, this is your heads-up. Found before the timeline.
stance: Godot is quietly becoming the engine indies pick first, not the fallback.

hook: Studios earn twice as much from digital vs physical sales
caption: Studios earn twice as much from digital sales, says the Lords of the Fallen CEO. The economics behind why physical game copies are quietly dying. The numbers my feed pulls out for you.
stance: Physical game sales are done, and studios have known it for years.

hook: DeFi has huge brand awareness and almost zero actual usage
caption: DeFi's problem in one line: everyone's heard of it, almost nobody uses it. 694 people surveyed, the gap is brutal. The uncomfortable data my feed keeps over the hype.
stance: DeFi's real bottleneck isn't tech. It's that awareness never converted to usage.

hook: The experiment that proved 'spooky action' is real.
caption: Quantum theory said particles could affect each other instantly. This experiment proved it. 47 likes, 44 replies still arguing. The stuff my feed keeps.

caption: CERN reports a new form of quantum superposition — one thing in two states at once, for real. Your intuition breaks, the math doesn't. The stuff my feed keeps over the memes.

caption: You can build a real quantum entanglement rig at home — Scientific American wrote the guide. Spooky action, on your desk. The kind of build my feed keeps for you.

caption: Quantum information theory could sharpen exoplanet imaging all the way to the theoretical limit — sharper alien-world photos from pure math. The space-tech find my feed keeps.

hook: The biggest Big Tech acquisitions, ranked.
caption: The largest deals Big Tech ever made — ranked in one place. Half the apps you open daily started as someone else's startup. The map my feed keeps so you see the whole board.

hook: The FTC is finally reopening old Big Tech deals.
caption: Years of untouched acquisitions — now the FTC wants a second look. If they unwind even one, the map changes. The regulatory stuff my feed keeps.

caption: Aurora's cash runway is closing and the only exits are spinout, layoffs, or absorption. This is how the AI shakeout actually ends. Found before the headlines.

caption: A myth-by-myth breakdown of what antitrust actually does — and doesn't — to Big Tech. Sharpens every hot take you're about to post. The stuff my feed keeps.

hook: Apple aside, Amazon Facebook and Google are (almost) all guilty
caption: Apple aside, the antitrust case says Amazon, Facebook and Google are (almost) all guilty. The platforms you build on are the ones on trial. Found before the timeline caught it.

hook: The DOJ's top antitrust cop says Big Tech looks like Standard Oil
caption: The DOJ's top antitrust cop says Big Tech now looks a lot like Standard Oil. When regulators reach for the monopoly playbook, your whole stack is in scope. The stuff my feed keeps.
stance: The Standard Oil comparison means breakups, not fines, are on the table.

hook: Everyone blamed Facebook. Maybe Apple was the problem all along.
caption: Everyone thought Facebook was the 800-pound gorilla. This argues Apple was the real monopoly all along. Contrarian take on who Big Tech antitrust should actually target. Found before the timeline.
stance: Apple's App Store lock-in is a bigger monopoly story than the ad giants.

hook: Biden's antitrust team is forming — Big Tech just got warned.
caption: Biden's antitrust team is taking shape and Big Tech got the warning shot. Regulation is coming for the giants you build on. The policy news my feed catches before the timeline.
stance: The next platform shift won't be a product — it'll be a court ruling.

hook: Congress promised to regulate Big Tech. It's quietly dying.
caption: 218 upvotes, 464 comments: Congress' push to regulate Big Tech is fizzling out. The giants outlasted the outrage again. The story my feed surfaces while the timeline moves on.
stance: Every Big Tech reckoning ends the same way: a hearing, a headline, then nothing.

hook: Big Tech is hiding the true cost of AI in its balance sheets.
caption: Hidden data center costs are quietly inflating Big Tech's operating income — legal, but it warps how you value AI. The accounting story my feed surfaced early.

title: Mothers Who Fight For Their Young
caption: Attenborough narrating mothers hunting eggs for their cubs and rescuing young from abductors. Nobody does the emotional whiplash of nature like BBC Earth. Kept from ~5,000 posts today.

title: Tiger vs A Wolf Pack In Siberia
caption: A Siberian tiger surrounded by a wolf pack in the snow. Siberia is where the most brutal predators go to refuge, and it shows. Who wins? Kept from ~5,000 posts today.

title: An Elephant's Walk Across Africa
caption: Giraffes fighting for dominance, hatchlings racing for their first breath, an elephant crossing a sea of gazelle. Africa in one frame. Threadverse pulled this from the noise.

title: Evolution's Weirdest Flex: The Platypus
caption: A duck's bill, a beaver's tail, venomous spurs, and it lays eggs. Attenborough narrating Tasmania's strangest mammal is peak comfort viewing. What animal breaks your brain most?

title: The Dinosaur Bird of the Congo
caption: The shoebill stork looks like a dinosaur that forgot to go extinct, and it hunts baby crocodiles. The Congo doesn't play. Would you get within 10 feet of this thing?

title: Two Lions vs One Angry Buffalo
caption: In the wilderness of Niassa, survival is a full-time race. Two lions, one cornered buffalo, and nothing decided until the last second. Who are you betting on?

title: The Ghosts of China's Mountains
caption: Golden fox cubs, hidden kingdoms, creatures most people will never see. Nat Geo calls them the ghosts of China's mountains. Threadverse kept this one out of 5,000.

title: A Cougar Under a Volcano
caption: Under the snowy crest of the High Cascades sit the most active volcanoes in the Lower 48, and a mountain lion prowls right through them. America the Beautiful, indeed.
